title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 4 RECAP
content: Week 4 was a tale of two halves. Donnie Dwyer put on an all-time performance for White in a blowout over Tan, Orange held off a furious Gray rally to stay unbeaten, Pink flipped the script on Red with a dominant second half, Purple bulldozed Blue with an historic 61-point second half, and Black survived a Yellow push to close out a tight one. Let's get into the action.

games.text: White came to play, and they never let up. White jumped out of the gate behind Donnie Dwyer, who was simply impossible to stop. Dwyer scored from everywhere on the floor, drawing fouls and knocking down threes in a first half that had Tan scrambling for answers. Matt Riordan ran the offense with precision, distributing the ball and crashing the glass while Zaire Wilson added points and playmaking on the perimeter. By the break, White had built a comfortable lead and showed no signs of slowing down. Halftime: White 38, Tan 27. Tan needed a big second half to get back in this one. It never came. White came out of the locker room and buried Tan entirely. Dwyer continued his historic night, finishing with 46 points on 18-of-34 shooting to go with 18 rebounds. Riordan finished with a near triple-double at 19 points, 13 rebounds, and 10 assists. Wilson added 20 points, 15 rebounds, and 9 assists. White's second half outscoring of 63-25 says everything. Tan's Kyle Picard led his team with 25 points, but it was not nearly enough. White won, 101-52.

performers.White: Donnie Dwyer — 46 pts, 18 REB, 1 AST; Zaire Wilson — 20 pts, 15 REB, 9 AST, 3 STL; Matt Riordan — 19 pts, 13 REB, 10 AST, 2 STL
performers.Tan: Kyle Picard — 25 pts, 9 REB, 2 AST, 2 STL; Cory Chaplin — 11 pts, 3 REB, 4 AST, 1 STL; Aiden Brown — 10 pts, 4 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL

games.text: Orange and Gray gave us a battle from tip to buzzer. Orange came out firing, pushing the pace and knocking down threes to build an early lead. Trent Roleau was a consistent scoring threat, going 10-of-19 from the field and 4-of-9 from three on his way to 26 first-half points. Cole Sampson shot the lights out from deep, going 5-of-8 from three en route to 21 points. Will Oboh was active on both ends, scoring 20 and keeping Gray's guards uncomfortable all game. Halftime: Orange 55, Gray 50. Gray had cut into the lead and was very much alive. The second half saw Gray's two subs carry the load. Sub Finn was a one-man wrecking crew, finishing with 39 points on 16-of-29 shooting, 18 rebounds, and 7 assists. Sub Peter added 24 points and six rebounds. Drew Fisher was a force on the boards with 12 rebounds and 13 points. But despite the heroic effort, Gray couldn't fully close the gap. Orange held on behind balanced contributions and held Gray at bay down the stretch. Orange won, 101-91.

performers.Orange: Trent Roleau — 26 pts, 3 REB, 1 STL; Cole Sampson — 21 pts, 4 REB, 4 AST; Will Oboh — 20 pts, 5 REB, 6 AST, 3 STL
performers.Gray: Sub Finn — 39 pts, 18 REB, 7 AST, 2 STL; Sub Peter — 24 pts, 6 REB, 1 AST; Drew Fisher — 13 pts, 12 REB, 4 AST

games.text: Red had the lead. Pink had the fourth quarter. Red controlled the first half behind Josh Carter, who was relentless attacking the basket and stretching the defense with his shooting. Will Ewald contributed 24 points and 13 rebounds while battling in the paint. Red's offense clicked early, and they built a six-point cushion heading into the break. Halftime: Red 56, Pink 50. All Pink needed was a reset. The second half belonged to Pink, and it wasn't close. Tyrone Conley took over the game, finishing with 27 points, 13 rebounds, and 8 assists. Kyle Haley was an absolute force, pulling down 21 rebounds to go with 28 points and 7-of-13 shooting from three. Liam Walsh hit big shots and drew fouls, finishing with 24 points. Pink outscored Red 49-29 in the second half to complete the comeback. Pink won, 99-85.

performers.Pink: Kyle Haley — 28 pts, 21 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Tyrone Conley — 27 pts, 13 REB, 8 AST, 1 STL; Liam Walsh — 24 pts, 6 REB, 5 AST, 3 STL
performers.Red: Josh Carter — 34 pts, 6 REB, 2 STL; Will Ewald — 24 pts, 13 REB, 4 AST, 2 STL; Kolby Bradford — 12 pts, 2 REB, 4 AST, 2 STL

games.text: Purple did not come to mess around. Purple built their lead methodically in the first half, getting contributions up and down the roster. Brandon Huestis hit four threes to keep Blue's defense stretched. Brock Carpenter dominated the glass with 11 rebounds. And Tre Grubbs was automatic inside, shooting 11-of-16 from the field and knocking down five threes on his way to 27 points. Trevis Buckhannon brought a physical presence that Blue could not handle. Halftime: Purple 46, Blue 35. Blue needed to find something. They could not. Purple's second half was one for the record books. They outscored Blue 61-26, effectively ending any drama early in the third. Trevis Buckhannon hauled in 20 rebounds to go with 23 points. Grubbs finished as the team's leading scorer. For Blue, Joe Ruggles provided a bright spot with 19 points and five threes, and Nick Lynn chipped in 18 points and 11 rebounds, but the deficit was simply too steep. Purple won, 107-61.

performers.Purple: Tre Grubbs — 27 pts, 12 REB, 1 AST; Trevis Buckhannon — 23 pts, 20 REB, 1 STL; Brandon Huestis — 20 pts, 2 REB, 4 AST
performers.Blue: Joe Ruggles — 19 pts, 4 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Nick Lynn — 18 pts, 11 REB, 2 AST, 2 STL; Matt Spear — 9 pts, 4 REB, 3 AST, 2 STL

games.text: Black and Yellow kept it close all night, but Stefan Radan made sure Black went home with the win. Black held a slim advantage throughout the first half. Stefan Radan was virtually unstoppable, getting to his spots and converting at a high rate. Chris Cayole ran the offense and got into the paint with regularity. For Yellow, Alex Philo shot it well from three and Logan Letourneau was a jack of all trades, scoring, rebounding, and defending. Halftime: Black 38, Yellow 36. Two points separated these teams. Neither squad flinched. The second half played out similarly, with both teams trading baskets. Radan was the difference maker, finishing with 38 points on 15-of-25 shooting and 6-of-11 from three. Cayole added 27 points, 9 rebounds, and 7 assists to keep the offense moving. Yellow's Letourneau finished with 23 points, 10 rebounds, and 4 steals, and Philo added 24 points. But Black's depth and balance proved to be just enough. Black won, 90-84.

performers.Black: Stefan Radan — 38 pts, 8 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Chris Cayole — 27 pts, 9 REB, 7 AST, 2 STL; Kenny Black — 12 pts, 9 REB, 2 AST, 1 STL
performers.Yellow: Alex Philo — 24 pts, 7 REB; Logan Letourneau — 23 pts, 10 REB, 5 AST, 4 STL; Drew Pelkey — 13 pts, 5 REB, 1 AST

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: This recap is proudly brought to you by the Alta Group and The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub. Thank you for your continued support of the Revolution Men's League.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is also brought to you by Warden's Wisdom — a mindfulness-based coaching business dedicated to helping individuals relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices. Whether you're navigating a comeback or just trying to stay locked in, Warden's Wisdom has something for you.
sponsors.quote: The present moment is the only place where the game is actually played. Stay there.

closing: We'll see you on the court next week. Tune in for more action, more big performances, and more reasons to love this league.
